_comment: Template registry for prep_features_public.py (the orchestrator). ADJUST paths, flags, and
output specs to your environment. Scripts not listed here are auto-discovered and run
with DEFAULT_CMD_TEMPLATE; output files not described here fall back to inference
(join keys from CANDIDATE_JOIN_KEYS, every numeric/boolean non-key column shipped).
Command tokens are substituted with {python} {script} {input} {output} {work} {scripts}.
{input} is the input-data root; reference topic subfolders as {input}/<topic>.
{output} is this script's own (empty) output subdir, used to attribute collected tables.
NOTE: service_demographics_by_route.csv is intentionally NOT described below. Its column
set varies with which Census tables were supplied (commute/income/vehicles/etc.), and a
described table whose keep_cols are not all present is skipped wholesale; inference ships
whatever numeric columns it produced, including the commute counts/shares from ACS S0801.
NOTE: gtfs_route_features.csv keep_cols deliberately EXCLUDE revenue_hours/revenue_miles.
Those are NTD-sourced supply quantities that live on the anchor (the NTD figures are more
accurate than the schedule-derived GTFS copy), and prep_features_public governance forbids them
from any feature bundle; without an explicit keep_cols, inference would ship them and the
run would fail closed.
NOTE: route_transfer_calculator.py also writes route_transfers_detail.csv (one row per
target/connector pair). It is deliberately not described: it has no route_id column, so
inference finds no join key and skips it. Only the route_id-keyed summary ships, and only
transfer_route_count (the transfer_routes column is a comma-separated string, not a
predictor). The script defaults to weekday (non-holiday) service via its --day filter.
NOTE: private_shuttle_coverage_by_route_gpd.py cleans the agency's private-shuttle operator
registry ({input}/shuttles/private_shuttles.csv) and rolls shuttle presence up per route so
the models can test whether private shuttles move ridership. Its other outputs are handled
correctly without being described: private_shuttles_clean.csv and
private_shuttles_needs_geocoding.csv carry no candidate join key, so inference skips them,
and Private_Shuttle_Stops.zip is not a csv/xlsx so it is never collected (drop that zip
under {input}/shapefiles to also include shuttles in the POI coverage counts).
NOTE: the CaBi chain is three stages. bikeshare_ridership_trends.py reads the monthly trip
extracts under {input}/cabi and writes station_daytype_ridership.csv (weekday/Saturday/
Sunday average ridership per station; observed federal holidays count as Sunday-equivalent,
so avg_weekday_riders is non-holiday weekdays, matching the GTFS-side features). Its tables
are month- or station_id-keyed, so inference rightly skips them all; only
cabi_coverage_by_route_gpd.py's route rollup ships. gbfs_ridership_join.py writes enriched
station GeoJSON for mapping only (never collected; the collector gathers csv/xlsx).
